CheckTitle: AI Agent LLM Model Must Receive Security Updates Within 30 Days
CheckType: Software and Configuration Checks/AI Agent Security Best Practices
Description: **AI Agent Supply Chain Security**. Validates that the LLM model used by each AI agent has a defined update cadence of 30 days or fewer. Stale models may contain known vulnerabilities, alignment regressions, or unpatched prompt injection weaknesses that have been fixed in newer versions.
Risk: An LLM model without a defined or enforced update cadence will accumulate security debt. Newly discovered vulnerabilities in model weights, tokenizers, or inference code may remain exploitable indefinitely if no update process is defined.

Code.Other:
1. Define a formal model update policy with a maximum cadence of 30 days.
2. Subscribe to security advisories for the model provider or open-source repository.
3. Automate model version checks in the CI/CD pipeline and alert when a newer secure version is available.
4. Test updated models against a regression suite before promoting to production.

Recommendation.Text: Establish a model update policy with a maximum 30-day cadence. Subscribe to model security advisories and automate update checks in the deployment pipeline.

Notes: ASPM-075 check from the Prowler ASPM check suite.
